sutta: monks let a monk cultivate a friend whose ways are 7 what 7 he gives what is hard to give he does what is hard to do bears what is hard to bear confesses his own secret keeps others in want forsakes 1 not despises not when 1 is ruined verily monks let a monk cultivate a friend whose ways are such as here although here this is advice to a monk to look for a friend with these qualities but it also applies equally to lay people if you want to look for a friend now a friend with these qualities is a worthy friend 1 he gives what is hard to give and then he does what is hard to do then he bears what is hard to bear and then he confesses his secret to you and then when you confess your secret to him he keeps your secret he doesn't divulge it to others and then when you need him he does not forsake you sometimes when we are down and out we need a friend a good friend will still come to you when you are down and out but i'm not a good friend when you are down he will run away then the last 1 he despises not when you are ruined when you are down and out now he does not despise you he still looks to you as a friend but most people when you are down and outnight they run away then the nexus
